**In Revit**
- Create a collaboration view for export
- Create an export setup using internal coordinates and units
- Export the model to DWG format using the created setup
- Identify which two points will be used for shared reference point coordination
- Both points must have the
- same elevation
- for correct results
- These points
- should not change throughout the life
- of the project. Fixed Gride lines or existing property boundary corners are good choice
- Transformed coordination in dynamo for points from Civil3D to Revit

_Captured Friday, 22 November 2024 10:10 pm · Computational › Dynamo_

- CES226103-L - IntertrackWall ADPT 2P Assembly creation
- New> Metric Generic Model Adaptive
- Place two point in random place> Make adaptive
- Set working plane> Pick the horizontal plane of the adaptive points> Put two new points at each adaptive points
- Pick one point at each reference point and offset them by 1000 and -1000
- Pick top and bottom points and create Spline through points
- Select the line and change it to reference line
- Place two new points on each reference lines
- Aline the new points with the adaptive points
- Select the new point> Click on the Host Point By Intersection> Click on the adaptive point on other reference line
- Do same for other point
- Pick two opposite points and draw spline

_Captured Monday, 9 December 2024 4:36 pm · Computational › Dynamo_

- To define timber pole set-out locations relative to a feature line in Civil 3D using Dynamo, follow these steps:
- Step 1: Prepare the Feature Line in Civil 3D
- Ensure the feature line is properly defined and has the correct elevations in Civil 3D.
- Name the feature line for easier identification in Dynamo.
- Step 2: Set Up Dynamo for Civil 3D
- Open Dynamo for Civil 3D.
- Load the
- Civil 3D Toolkit
- package (if not already installed) from the Dynamo Package Manager. This provides nodes for interacting with Civil 3D elements.
- Step 3: Import the Feature Line
- Use the Select Civil 3D Feature Line node to select the feature line from Civil 3D.
- Extract the geometry of the feature line using Feature Line.GetGeometry.
- Retrieve the points along the feature line using Feature Line.Points.
- Step 4: Define Pile Spacing
- Decide on the spacing for the timber poles (e.g., every 2 meters along the feature line).
- Use a Range node to generate intervals along the length of the feature line.
- Step 5: Locate Set-Out Points
- Use the Curve.PointAtParameter node to calculate points along the feature line at specific intervals. The parameter input is based on a normalized value (0 to 1), so adjust your spacing accordingly.
- Convert real-world distances to normalized values using Curve.Length and appropriate calcu
